content.full_text:
Re
Farn Porrrrcnl PnacrrcEs CoupussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5ffi . Fax (916) 322-0886
October 21,2004
James V. Lacy
Wewer &Lacy,LLP
Civic Center Plaza
30011 Ivy Glenn Drive, Suite 223
LagunaNiguel, CA92677
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-04-195
Dear Mr- Lacy:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of "i$o New Taxes, a Project of
the Small Business Action Committee and the Howard Jarvis Tanpayers Association"
(\INT') for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
'Act').1
QUESTTON
Does NNT, a ballot measure committee, need to disclose contributors to the "No
on PropositionTZ" committee when paylng for communications that in whole or in part
oppose Proposition 72?
CONCLUSION
No. Regardless of whether a given political advertisernent is a contribution to
"No on Proposition '72" or an independent expenditure, NNT is required to disclose only
the appropriate contributors to NNT since NNT is payrng for the political
communication-
FACTS
No New Taxes, a Project of the Small Business Action Committee and the
Howard Jaruis Taxpayers Association intends to address ballot measures in more than a
single election. For the November 2004 eleclion, it intends to oppose four statewide
ballot initiatives, namely Propositions 63, 65, 67 and 72. NNT has complied with the
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
Lqgl


File No. A-04-195
Page No. 2
requirements of the Act, namely it previously filed a Form 410 Statement of
Organization. When it qualifies it will file an amended Form 410, andperiodic Form 460
disclosures consistent with law.
NNT intends to engage in its activityin the November2004 election independent
of any other ballot measure committee. As such, Small Business Action Committee
('SBAC') has intended to engage in independent expenditme activity in opposition to
such statewide ballot initiatives, and to prepare and file the proper additional disclosures.
The creation of the project committee is entirely the decision and action of the qtonsoring
organizations and neither the sponsoring organizations, nor any of their stafi, directors,
officers and agents, or consultants have acted in concert with any otherperson, including
any other ballot measure committee, to create NNT.
Howev€r, one ofNNT's sponsoringorganizations, namely, the Small Business
Action Committee (of which you are also general cormsel) has recentlymade a decision
to hire a consulting finn based in Sacramento for organization maragernent prposes that
is ownd and operated by Jeffiey Flint. Mr. Flin! you have leamed, has a nurrber of
differ€nt clients, and is also a consultant to the "l.Io on hop. T2"ballotmeasure
committee, where he assists that orgarrizationin ooalition building As a manager of
SBAC, Mr. Flint would have substantial influence overthe activities ofNNT. Members
of SBAC's Board of Directors, named Joel Fox and Chris George, also wish to join a
voluntary advisory committee to the 'T.lo on Prop.72- committee where campaign
skategy will be discussed.
Under regulation 18225.7(c)(3XA), you note that coordination with a ballot
mea$lre committee is presurned where SBAC's project committee'?etains the services
of a person who provides either the candidate or the comrnittee suppoiling or opposing
the ballot mea$lre with professional services related to cmrpaign or fimdraising strategy
for that same election."
NNT intends to engage in advertising expenditures where its position on all four
measures will jointly appear. For Propositions 63, 65 and 67, this activity will be by
independent expenditure. For Proposition TL,yoabelieve the support would likely be
viewed by the FPPC as coordinated.
Reading Government code section 84505 and regulation 18225-7,
subdivision(c)(3[A), together, you are concerned about the application of section 84503
to the prqect committee's advertising and seek advice on what the proper disclosure on
advertising for the project committee should be under the facts stated herein.
Specifically, you ask whether you are required to disclose the top two contributors to the
'T'Io on Proposition 72" corttrrtittee in addition to disclosing pertinent contributors to
NNT.


File No. A-04-195
Page No. 3
ANALYSIS
Sections 84501-8451I are generally described as the advertising disclosure
stafutes, which, as the nitme implies, regulates the content of disclosures to be made by
those who pay forpolitical advertisements- As you correctly assumg these statutes
govern the tlpes of communications about which you inquire - specifically, NNT's
political advertisements urging voters to vote "no" on certain ballot measures in the
upcoming Novemb€r election.
Regulation 18225.7, on the other hand, defines the term 'tnade at the behest,',
which may determine whether a given expenditure (not only for advertising) shall be
considered a conkibution or an independent experlditure. (See g 82015, rrbd. (bX2),
stating that "[a] payment made at the behest of a candidate is a contribution to the
candidate unless [certainJ--. criteria--.are satisfied).) Thus, in determining whether
political advertisements paid forbyNNT are contributions to'tlo on Proposition 72,, or
independent expenditmes for reporting putposes, we look to regulation 18225.7 to assist
in answering that question.
The question you ask, however, does not implicateregulation 18225-7- Instead,
the question of which donors are disclosed in an advertisement is governed by sections
84503 and 84506 (if the exper:rditure is an independent expenditure). Under either
scenario, the critical question to be answered is which committeg NNT or.T.{o on
Proposition 72," paid for the communication- In yorn facts, you indicate the
communications will bepaid forbyNNT, not any other committee. Therefore, in the
political advertisements you describe, NNT need disclose only the top two (if applicable)
donors of $50,000 or more (cumulatively) to NNT.2
If you have any other questions regarding this mattetr, please contact me at (916)
322-s66A.
Sincerely,
Luisa
General
By: C. Scott Tocher
Senior Cormsel, Irgal Division
CSTjg
I:\Adviceltrs\04-195
' Please note that this letter does not address whether NNT is a general pu{pose or primarily
formed committee. (Sections 82027 .5 and 82047 .5.)
